Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search for Vectral stipend records in Airtable
Agent searches airtable_search_records(base_id: "...", table_name: "Stipends", field_name: "Account", value: "Vectral Systems") or airtable_list_records(base_id: "...", table_name: "Stipends") and filters for Vectral. Alternative: airtable_search_records(base_id: "...", table_name: "Stipends", field_name: "Employee", value: "Maria Santos") (known affected employee). Discovery: Vectral stipend records with three still marked "Rejected" due to stale default policy limits — Maria Santos's $550 standing desk, James Okafor's $82 ergonomic keyboard tray, and Lisa Park's $130 mesh WiFi system. Note: Danielle Osei's $475 ergonomic chair and Kevin Tran's $185 internet setup were also initially auto-rejected but have already been manually approved by Mina Hashimoto. Rachel Whitfield complained about the false rejections.
OE 2: Search for Mosaic stipend records in Airtable
Agent searches airtable_search_records(base_id: "...", table_name: "Stipends", field_name: "Account", value: "Mosaic Robotics") or airtable_list_records(base_id: "...", table_name: "Stipends") and filters for Mosaic. Alternative: airtable_search_records(base_id: "...", table_name: "Stipends", field_name: "Employee", value: "Andre Washington"). Discovery: Mosaic stipend records with some marked "Approved" that shouldn't have been — the malformed JSON config let excluded categories through. Dmitri's audit established the total Mosaic financial exposure at $885 (Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk exceeding $800 cap by $400, Andre Washington's $350 coworking membership in an excluded category, and Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking day passes in an excluded category). Emeka Diallo confirmed this figure in internal discussions.
OE 3: Find Dmitri's accuracy audit findings
Agent searches conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Dmitri audit", filter_in_channel: #engineering) or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "accuracy audit"). Alternatives: search_emails(query: "ExpenseBot audit"), linear_list_issues(query: "ExpenseBot audit"). Discovery: Dmitri's audit found 83% accuracy rate (39/47 correct), meaning 8 transactions were incorrectly handled across both clients. Note: agent may also encounter Dmitri's separate data quality audit (stipend_fraud scenario — duplicate receipts from TechComfort Supply), which is a different issue from the config bugs.
OE 4: Find Marcus's internal discussion about financial exposure
Agent searches conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Marcus out-of-policy", filter_in_channel: #finance) or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "financial exposure ExpenseBot"). Alternatives: search_emails(query: "Marcus out-of-policy approvals"), conversations_search_messages(search_query: "$885"). Discovery: Marcus raised financial concerns about Mosaic's out-of-policy approvals, initially flagging $1,550 in exposure. After Dmitri's full audit, the revised figure is $885. Emeka confirmed the $885 figure. Agent must verify Airtable totals match Marcus's figures and note any discrepancy between the initial $1,550 estimate and the audited $885.
OE 5: Identify contact details for write actions
Agent uses contacts_search_contacts(query: "Mina Hashimoto"), contacts_search_contacts(query: "Emeka Diallo"), contacts_search_contacts(query: "Samira Tariq"), contacts_search_contacts(query: "Marcus Thorne"). Contact details may also be discovered from previous Slack/Email search results.
OE 6: Update Mosaic stipend records to "Held"
Agent uses airtable_update_records for each Mosaic stipend record that was incorrectly approved. Changes status from "Approved" to "Held" so finance can review before payout.
OE 7: Update Vectral stipend records to "Under Review"
Agent uses airtable_update_records for each Vectral stipend record that was incorrectly rejected. Changes status from "Rejected" to "Under Review" for reprocessing.
OE 8: Email Mina and Emeka about the status changes
Agent uses send_email to Mina Hashimoto (Vectral AM) and Emeka Diallo (Mosaic AM) with a breakdown of which records were changed for their respective clients. They need this to manage fallout with Rachel Whitfield (Vectral) and Tamara Byrd (Mosaic).
OE 9: Add comments to existing Linear tickets
Agent first uses linear_list_issues(query: "ExpenseBot") or linear_list_issues(query: "config remediation") to find existing tickets, then linear_create_comment on relevant tickets listing the financial impact: which records were affected and dollar amounts.
OE 10: Create financial reconciliation tracking ticket (conditional)
If no existing ticket tracks the financial reconciliation, agent uses linear_create_issue to create one and assigns it to Marcus Thorne so finance owns the follow-through.
OE 11: Post audit summary in #finance
Agent uses conversations_add_message(channel_id: "C005" (#finance)) to post a complete summary: total records affected per client, dollar exposure on each side, status changes made, and any discrepancies with Marcus's earlier figures. Visible to Marcus Thorne and Hana Kim.
OE 12: Message Samira with key findings
Agent uses send_email to Samira Tariq OR conversations_add_message (DM to Samira) with key findings: total records affected, dollar amounts on each side, and whether all impacted records have been corrected or if there are open items to flag for Elena.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The status of the incorrectly approved Mosaic Robotics stipend records was changed to "Held" in Airtable, covering the following 3 records: Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk, Andre Washington's $350 coworking space membership, and Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking day passes.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The trajectory confirms Airtable update calls changed Andre Washington's standing desk ($1,200), Andre Washington's coworking membership ($350), and Kenji Nakamura's coworking day passes ($135) to 'Held' status. All three required records were updated.
✓Run #2The agent called airtable_update_records at step [54] setting 'Approval Status' to 'Held' for recStip000000021 (Andre Washington Standing Desk $1,200), recStip000000022 (Andre Washington Coworking $350), and recStip000000023 (Kenji Nakamura Coworking Day Passes $135). All 3 required Mosaic records were updated.
✓Run #3The agent called airtable_update_records and changed Andre Washington's Standing Desk ($1,200), Andre Washington's Coworking Space Membership ($350), and Kenji Nakamura's Coworking Day Passes ($135) to 'Held' status. The tool call was made even though Airtable returned empty results earlier, so the record IDs appear to be inferred/fabricated—but the update call was made with the correct 3 records.
✓Run #4The trajectory confirms airtable_update_records was called updating Andre Washington's standing desk ($1,200), Andre Washington's coworking membership ($350), and Kenji Nakamura's coworking day passes ($135) to 'Held' status in tblStipends00001.
✓Run #5The agent called airtable_update_records and successfully updated 5 Mosaic records to 'Held' status, including recStip000000021 (Andre Washington standing desk $1,200), recStip000000022 (Andre Washington coworking $350), recStip000000023 (Kenji Nakamura coworking $135), plus duplicate tracking records rec_stipend_andre_desk and rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork. All 3 unique required transactions are covered.
✓Run #6The agent called `airtable_update_records` with status 'Held' for all three Mosaic records: Andre Washington's standing desk (recStip000000021/rec_stipend_andre_desk), Andre Washington's coworking membership (recStip000000022), and Kenji Nakamura's coworking day passes (recStip000000023/rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork). All three were successfully updated.
2 The status of incorrectly rejected Vectral Systems stipend records was changed to "Under Review" in Airtable, covering at least the following records still marked as Rejected: Maria Santos's $550 standing desk, James Okafor's $82 ergonomic keyboard tray, and Lisa Park's $130 mesh WiFi system.
outcome

The 2 already manually corrected records (Danielle Osei, Kevin Tran) were not changed, which is acceptable.

All 3 required still-pending Vectral records were updated.
✓Run #3The agent called airtable_update_records and changed Maria Santos's Standing Desk ($550), James Okafor's Ergonomic Keyboard Tray ($82), and Lisa Park's Mesh WiFi System ($130) to 'Under Review' status, matching the required records.

The 3 required still-rejected records were covered.
✓Run #6The agent called `airtable_update_records` with status 'Under Review' for Maria Santos (recStip000000018/rec_stipend_maria_santos_desk), James Okafor (recStip000000019), and Lisa Park (recStip000000020/rec_stipend_lisa_park_wifi). All three were successfully updated.
3 An email was sent to Mina Hashimoto ([mina.hashimoto@moveops.com](mailto:mina.hashimoto@moveops.com)) containing a breakdown of the Vectral stipend record changes.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #3An email was sent to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com with the subject 'ExpenseBot Reconciliation — Vectral Systems: Impacted Stipend Records Updated' including a detailed breakdown of the 3 Vectral records incorrectly rejected.

✓Run #5The agent sent an email to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com with a detailed breakdown of all 3 Vectral records changed to 'Under Review', plus context about the already-corrected records and implications for her client Rachel Whitfield.

4 An email was sent to Emeka Diallo ([emeka.diallo@moveops.com](mailto:emeka.diallo@moveops.com)) containing a breakdown of the Mosaic stipend record changes.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #3An email was sent to emeka.diallo@moveops.com with the subject 'ExpenseBot Reconciliation — Mosaic Robotics: Impacted Stipend Records Updated' including a breakdown of the 3 Mosaic transactions incorrectly approved.

✓Run #5The agent sent an email to emeka.diallo@moveops.com with a detailed breakdown of all 3 Mosaic records changed to 'Held', including the two specific bugs and implications for Tamara Byrd at Mosaic.
✓Run #6The agent sent an email to emeka.diallo@moveops.com with a full breakdown of Mosaic Robotics stipend record changes, listing which records were moved to 'Held', the dollar exposure, and action items for managing fallout with Tamara.
5 A comment was added to at least one existing Linear ticket related to ExpenseBot.
outcome
 ✓✓—✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1Comments were added to at least 4 existing Linear tickets (ENG-215, ENG-214, and others) with financial reconciliation detail. The agent searched for and found existing ExpenseBot-related tickets before commenting.
✓Run #2The agent called linear_create_comment on four existing tickets: issue_audit_expenses [68], issue_p1_vectral_config [70], issue_p1_mosaic_malformed [72], and issue_pilot_monitoring [74] — all with financial impact details.
—Run #3No justification
✓Run #4The trajectory confirms linear_create_comment was called on issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216), ENG-214, and ENG-215 — three existing ExpenseBot-related tickets all received financial impact comments.
✓Run #5The agent called linear_create_comment on 'issue_p1_mosaic_malformed' and 'issue_p1_vectral_config'. The tool results confirmed successful creation (comment IDs returned: comment_1759d1dc... and comment_95c9689d...), meaning both existing tickets received comments.
✓Run #6The agent added comments to three existing Linear tickets: issue_p1_mosaic_malformed, issue_p1_vectral_config, and issue_audit_expenses. All comment creation calls returned success with comment IDs.
6 The comment added to the ExpenseBot Linear ticket lists affected employees or records by name, including the following: Andre Washington and Kenji Nakamura (Mosaic incorrect approvals), Danielle Osei, Maria Santos, James Okafor, Kevin Tran, and Lisa Park (Vectral incorrect rejections)."
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows per-employee financial reconciliation tables were created for ENG-215 (Mosaic: Andre Washington, Kenji Nakamura) and ENG-214 (Vectral: 5 records). The '$660 already corrected' figure implicitly covers Danielle Osei and Kevin Tran. The table format with per-employee amounts strongly implies names were included.
✓Run #2The comprehensive comment at step [68] on issue_audit_expenses lists tables containing Andre Washington, Kenji Nakamura for Mosaic, and the Vectral section includes Maria Santos, James Okafor, and continues (truncated). From the linear_create_issue at [76] and #finance post at [78], Danielle Osei ($475) and Kevin Tran ($185) are also included in the 'Previously corrected' section. All 7 required employees are covered across the comment chain.
✓Run #3The Linear comments included full tables of affected records. Mosaic comments listed Andre Washington and Kenji Nakamura; Vectral comments listed Maria Santos, James Okafor, Lisa Park, and noted Danielle Osei ($475) and Kevin Tran ($185) as previously corrected records.
✓Run #4The ENG-214 comment lists Danielle Osei ($475), Kevin Tran ($185), Maria Santos ($550), James Okafor ($82), and Lisa Park ($130); ENG-215 lists Andre Washington and Kenji Nakamura. All 7 employees named in the criteria are covered across the comments.
✓Run #5The Mosaic comment lists Andre Washington and Kenji Nakamura by name. The Vectral comment lists Maria Santos, James Okafor, Lisa Park by name, and also mentions Danielle Osei and Kevin Tran as 'Previously Corrected.' All required employees are named across the two comments.
✓Run #6The Linear comments explicitly list Andre Washington and Kenji Nakamura (Mosaic) as well as Danielle Osei, Kevin Tran, Maria Santos, James Okafor, and Lisa Park (Vectral) by name with record IDs and dollar amounts. All required employees are named.
7 The comment added to the ExpenseBot Linear ticket lists dollar amounts involved, including approximately $885 for the Mosaic incorrect approvals (Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk with $400 over the $800 cap, Andre Washington's $350 coworking membership, Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking day passes) and approximately $1,422 for the Vectral incorrect rejections (Danielle Osei's $475 ergonomic chair, Kevin Tran's $185 internet setup, Maria Santos's $550 standing desk, James Okafor's $82 keyboard tray, Lisa Park's $130 mesh WiFi system).
outcome
 ✗✓✗✓✗✗ 33%
✗Run #1The agent used $1,685 as the Mosaic total (full $1,200 standing desk + $350 + $135) instead of ~$885 (only the $400 overage + $350 + $135). The correct exposure is only the amount above policy ($400 overage), not the full transaction value. The criterion expected ~$885, not $1,685.
✓Run #2The comment at [68] includes specific dollar amounts: $1,200, $350, $135 for Mosaic and the Vectral section with amounts. It states '$885 net out-of-policy exposure' and '$1,422 total Vectral false rejection exposure', and the issue_pilot_monitoring comment [74] also includes dollar amounts for both sides.
✗Run #3The criterion requires the comment to list Mosaic incorrect approvals as approximately $885 (counting only the $400 overage on Andre's desk, not the full $1,200). The agent instead reported $1,685 (counting full transaction amounts), and the Vectral total as $762 still pending rather than $1,422 total. The dollar amounts do not match the ground truth.
✓Run #4The Linear comments include dollar amounts for all records: Mosaic actual exposure $885 (with breakdown: $400 desk overage, $350 coworking membership, $135 day passes) and Vectral $1,422 total (5 records itemized). All required amounts are present.
✗Run #5The Mosaic comment does state $885 as the out-of-policy overage breakdown. However, the Vectral comment shows 'Total incorrectly rejected: $762' for only the 3 still-pending records, and lists Danielle Osei ($475) and Kevin Tran ($185) as 'Previously Corrected' separately — without presenting the combined ~$1,422 total. The criterion requires the comment to list approximately $1,422 for Vectral, which is not explicitly stated.

14 The #finance post (or agent's final report) identifies a discrepancy between Marcus Thorne's initially flagged exposure of $1,550 and the audited figure of approximately $885, noting that Marcus's original estimate was higher because it counted Andre Washington's full $1,200 standing desk rather than just the $400 overage, and did not yet include Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking passes.

The agent's analysis was inverted and incorrect.
✗Run #2The agent identifies a discrepancy between Marcus's $1,550 and $1,685 (actual total transaction value), noting the $135 gap is Kenji Nakamura's missing passes. However, the criteria expects the discrepancy to be between $1,550 and ~$885 (the net out-of-policy amount), with the explanation that Marcus counted the full $1,200 desk value rather than just the $400 policy overage. The agent's framing is incorrect — it compares total transaction values instead of policy-violation exposure amounts, and omits the key explanation about the full-desk-vs-overage counting error.
✗Run #3The agent identified a discrepancy between Marcus's $1,550 and its own figure of $1,685 (a $135 gap attributed to Kenji Nakamura's coworking passes). However, the ground truth discrepancy is between Marcus's $1,550 and the correct audited figure of ~$885 (only counting overages). The agent's comparison used wrong base figures and misattributed the nature of the gap—it missed that Marcus over-counted the desk transaction (full $1,200 vs. only the $400 overage).
✓Run #4The agent explicitly identifies the discrepancy between Marcus's $1,550 figure and the audited $885, explaining that Marcus counted the full $1,200 standing desk instead of only the $400 overage, and didn't include Kenji Nakamura's $135. This was documented in the finance channel post per the agent's final summary.

The agent never identifies the $885 vs $1,550 discrepancy or explains the overcounting of the desk's full price.
✗Run #6The agent identified a discrepancy between Marcus's $1,550 and a computed $1,685, attributing the $135 gap to the Kenji Nakamura coworking passes missing from Marcus's alert. However, the correct ground truth is that Marcus's $1,550 was high because he counted the full $1,200 desk (not just the $400 overage), while the actual exposure is ~$885.
